perturbation.original_text: For all purposes of this Agreement, the term “ Confidential Information ” shall collectively refer to all information or material disclosed or provided by the Company to Recipient, either orally or in writing, or obtained by Recipient from a third party or any other source, regardless of the manner in which it is furnished, concerning any aspect of the business or affairs of the Company or its “ affiliates ” (as such term is defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)).
perturbation.changed_text: For all purposes of this Agreement, the term “ Confidential Data ” shall collectively refer to all information or material disclosed or provided by the Company to Recipient, either orally or in writing, or obtained by Recipient from a third party or any other source, regardless of the manner in which it is furnished, concerning any aspect of the business or affairs of the Company or its “ affiliates ” (as such term is defined in Rule 12b-2 under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)).
perturbation.explanation: The change of the term 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' creates an in-text contradiction. Later sections reference 'Confidential Information'. This change implies two distinct categories of confidential data and confidential information when the document only defines “Confidential Information”. This contradicts the rest of the document where this term is utilized, making it unclear if the clauses apply to the 'Confidential Data'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall keep strictly confidential and shall not disclose, or cause or permit to be disclosed, to any person or entity, (i) any information about a potential transaction between Recipient and the Company (the “ Transaction ”) or the fact that Recipient has received, or may receive, Confidential Information and is considering the Transaction and all discussions between the Company and Recipient related thereto, including the existence of this Agreement, except that (subject to Section 2.2 below) Recipient may make such disclosure if it has received the reasonable written advice of its outside counsel that such disclosure must be made in order that Recipient not commit a violation of law, and (ii) the Confidential Information, except to those officers, employees or other authorized Representatives (as defined herein) and who shall agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement, and except as otherwise consented to in writing by the Company. Recipient shall take all actions reasonably necessary to ensure that the Confidential Information remains strictly confidential and is not disclosed to or seen, used or obtained by any person or entity except in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Recipient agrees not to contact any shareholders, directors, officers, employees, agents, customers, or suppliers of the Company or its affiliates with respect to the Transaction or for the purpose of obtaining information for use in evaluating the Transaction, without the Company’s prior written consent. Recipient further agrees that all inquiries, requests for information and other communications concerning the Transaction shall be made only through Guggenheim Securities, LLC (“Guggenheim”). Company agrees that, without Recipient’s prior written consent, it and its Representatives will not disclose to any other person the fact that Recipient is considering the Transaction, that this Agreement exists, that the Confidential Information has been made available to Recipient, that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning the Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect thereto including the status thereof, the valuation, or indicative offers, or proposals.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' because the latter explicitly references 'Confidential Information'. The modification creates an ambiguity as to whether the obligations outlined in the 'Contradicted Text' apply to the newly named 'Confidential Data', creating a misalignment of terminology in the document. According to the 'Perturbation Explanation', this contradiction arises because the rest of the document uses 'Confidential Information', making it unclear whether those clauses apply to the newly introduced term 'Confidential Data'.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees not to use any Confidential Information of the Company for any purpose except to evaluate and engage in discussions regarding the Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees not to utilize any Protected Information of the Company for any purpose except to evaluate and engage in discussions regarding the Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is changed to 'Protected Information' without defining 'Protected Information'. This raises uncertainty about whether the rest of document provisions that use 'Confidential Information' extend to 'Protected Information'. This in-text contradiction impacts the usability restrictions outlined in the original agreement. Specifically, it is now unclear if sections related to the return or ownership of 'Confidential Information' also apply to the 'Protected Information', leading to uncertain enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall, upon accomplishing the limited purpose of evaluating the Transaction, or at any time upon the request of the Company, immediately destroy or return to the Company all Confidential Information (including notes, writings and other material developed therefrom by Recipient) and all copies thereof and retain none for its files. Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither the Recipient nor its Representatives will be required to erase electronically stored Confidential Information that has been saved to a back-up file or other electronic medium in accordance with its or its Representatives’ ordinary back-up practices. Notwithstanding such return or destruction, Recipient shall continue to be bound by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' without defining 'Protected Information' creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which explicitly refers to 'Confidential Information'. The Recipient's obligations regarding the return or destruction of 'Confidential Information' are now unclear regarding 'Protected Information', rendering the enforcement of the agreement uncertain. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract now uses multiple terms (Confidential and Protected Information) without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges that it is aware, and agrees to advise its directors, officers, employees, agents and Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has material, non-public information concerning the Transaction from purchasing or selling securities of a company that may be a party to such Transaction or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges that it is aware, and agrees to advise its directors, officers, employees, agents and Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has relevant, inside information concerning the Transaction from purchasing or selling securities of a company that may be a party to such Transaction or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'material, non-public information' with 'relevant, inside information' creates ambiguity. While both phrases relate to privileged information, 'material, non-public information' has a specific legal definition under securities law. 'Relevant, inside information' is vaguer. This in-text contradiction weakens the enforceability of the clause, because it is unclear if the parties are held to SEC's definition of what is considered 'material'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledges that it is aware, and agrees to advise its directors, officers, employees, agents and Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has relevant, inside information concerning the Transaction from purchasing or selling securities of a company that may be a party to such Transaction or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'material, non-public information,' which has a specific legal definition. The changed text replaces this with 'relevant, inside information,' which is a vaguer term. The contradicted text contains the same vague wording, furthering the ambiguity. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear whether the parties are held to the SEC's definition of 'material' information, creating potential uncertainty regarding enforcement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be modified or amended and no provision hereof may be waived, in whole or in part, except by a written agreement signed by the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Understanding may not be modified or amended and no provision hereof may be waived, in whole or in part, except by a written agreement signed by the parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Understanding' in this section creates a direct contradiction with the rest of the document, which consistently refers to the document as an 'Agreement.' This inconsistency creates uncertainty about whether the modification restriction applies to the entire contract or just this specific 'Understanding' portion, therefore causing in-text contradiction within section 14.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Understanding may not be modified or amended and no provision hereof may be waived, in whole or in part, except by a written agreement signed by the parties hereto. No waiver of any breach or default hereunder shall be considered valid unless in writing, and no such waiver shall be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach or default of the same or similar nature. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, but all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'Understanding' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the Contradicted Text. While the Contradicted Text does use 'Understanding', it also uses 'Agreement' in the third sentence. Changing 'Agreement' to 'Understanding' creates ambiguity as to whether the entire document is an 'Agreement' or an 'Understanding', causing an in-text contradiction as it is uncertain to which document the clause applies. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
